behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-model diarization/label quality, not just wrapper code. A stubbed STT response would hide mislabeled or dropped speakers (MOCK-HIDE yes). No OS/subprocess requirement beyond API call, and this behavior is about model output correctness (LLM-DEP/ML-DEP yes), so Agentic is the minimum. Rejected Workflow because the behavior targets a single model-output property (speaker labeling), not multi-agentic step correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT provider and assert transcript speaker labels are present and consistently mapped to distinct attendees in the produced transcript artifact used by summarization.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser transformation logic on transcript text/labels into a structured object. The described failure boundary is local extraction logic, reproducible with deterministic fixtures (MOCK-MISS no for cross-module dependency). No real OS/network/subprocess needed, and no dependence on real model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function transcript fixtures containing explicit assignment phrases and assert extracted objects contain correct assignee and task text fields in the summary-input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the real summarizer model deciding to emit per-attendee action items from transcript evidence. Deterministic mocks can force passing outputs and miss real omission failures (LLM-DEP yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Workflow because this assertion is on one summarization decision quality outcome, not a multi-agentic orchestration chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcripts with known attendee-task assignments, call the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text focuses on routing logic after decisions are already identified. That disqualifies higher levels: it is not asking whether the model can identify decisions, only where identified decisions are placed. This is deterministic mapping and can fail within one function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Given pre-identified decision items in intermediate form, execute routing/mapping function and assert they are written to `decisions` schema fields and excluded from generic narrative fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher levels were considered, but rejected because behavior explicitly says 'identified decisions', narrowing scope to local routing logic.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This validates end output semantic placement quality from transcript content, which depends on real model extraction/classification behavior. Stubbed outputs would mask missed or misplaced decisions (LLM-DEP yes). Rejected Integration because model judgment on what is a key decision and section placement is core to correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcript inputs containing known key decisions through the live summarizer and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` and absent as only narrative-only mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly multi-step orchestration across repeated STT+summarization calls and merge state across segments (MULTI-STEP yes). Failures often occur at handoffs/order/accumulation boundaries and model outputs per segment. Mocking model calls can hide merge/coherence regressions. System alone is insufficient because correctness spans multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, execute the production segmentation pipeline end-to-end (segment -> transcribe -> summarize per segment -> merge), and assert final structured summary is single, merged, and coherent with no segment loss/duplication.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets the full agent pipeline with real recording and autonomous completion. It includes multiple dependent agentic steps (transcribe then summarize then structured delivery), so Workflow is the true failure boundary. Rejected System because it would cover infra execution but can miss cross-step model-driven pipeline failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like job with a real meeting recording from upload through final delivery endpoint/storage, with real STT and summarizer enabled, and assert successful completion plus valid structured output artifact without human fixes.
